SNAP_WOOD.name: Snap-Wood
SNAP_WOOD.description: Brittle framing and kindling found throughout the silt.

secondaryDrops.message: A cloud of luminescent spores clings to your sleeves. Potency gathered.

RIVER_SLUDGE.name: River Silt
RIVER_SLUDGE.description: Fine mineral particles gathered from the creek bed. Useful as a binder.

secondaryDrops.message: Your fingers brush against a calcified fragment in the silt.

TENDER_VINES.name: Tender-Vines
TENDER_VINES.description: Primary binding and lashing for all structural work.
TENDER_VINES.struggleMessage: You tug against the stubborn fibers... They groan but hold.

secondaryDrops.message: A cloud of luminescent spores clings to your sleeves. Potency gathered.

BONE.name: Primal Bone
BONE.description: Bleached remains found deep within the silt. Exceptionally hard.

PEAT_MOSS.name: Peat Moss
PEAT_MOSS.description: Spongy insulation and flooring that drinks up dampness.

secondaryDrops.message: A cloud of luminescent spores clings to your sleeves. Potency gathered.

RIVER_STONE.name: River Stone
RIVER_STONE.description: Smooth basalt shaped by moving water. Useful for grinding or weighing.

secondaryDrops.message: A calcified fragment is embedded in the stone.

SALT_CRYSTAL.name: Silt-Salt
SALT_CRYSTAL.description: Sharp, grey mineral deposits found where moisture has evaporated. High potency.

secondaryDrops.message: The crystals hum as they crumble, releasing dense spores.

DOWNED_OAK.name: Downed Oak
DOWNED_OAK.description: Heavy structural logs that form the skeleton of true shelters. Hard to process.
DOWNED_OAK.struggleMessage: Stomping down on the heavy oak. Your legs ache as the wood splinters slowly.
DOWNED_OAK.successMessage: The heavy log yields a structural piece.

secondaryDrops.message: A cloud of luminescent spores clings to your sleeves.
You recover some dry splinters from the core.

SPORES.name: Luminous Spores
SPORES.description: Microscopic specks of biological potential. They thrum with a faint, green light.

STUMP.name: Old Stump
STUMP.description: A solid, ancient anchor in the earth. Serves as a reliable workstation.
STUMP.successMessage: The Old Stump is solid as a boulder. It will serve as a stable workstation.

TWINED_CORDAGE.name: Twined Cordage
TWINED_CORDAGE.description: Hand-woven fibers, essential for complex bindings.

DRY_KINDLING.name: Dry Kindling
DRY_KINDLING.description: Small, dry splinters of snap-wood used to start fire or as precise shims.

LEAN_TO.name: Primitive Lean-to
LEAN_TO.description: A crude shelter of snap-wood and vine. Barely keeps the resonance out.

WATTLE_HUT.name: Wattle-and-Daub Hut
WATTLE_HUT.description: A sturdy dome of oak-frame and vine, plastered with thick river silt.

MOSS_CRADLE.name: Moss Cradle
MOSS_CRADLE.description: A cultivated plot where moss and spores reclaim the silt.

WOVEN_COTTAGE.name: Woven Cottage
WOVEN_COTTAGE.description: A refined home using stable oak and heavy cordage. Requires workstation stability.

GARDEN_TROWEL.name: Garden Trowel
GARDEN_TROWEL.description: A crude digging tool. Required for deep extraction from mud and silt.

dense_thicket.name: Dense Thicket
dense_thicket.description: Low-lying brush and saplings. Yields Kindling and Fiber.

damp_hollow.name: Damp Hollow
damp_hollow.description: A wet basin where water collects in the silt. Yields Moss and Clay.

tangled_deepwood.name: Tangled Deepwood
tangled_deepwood.description: The oldest, densest part of the forest. Heavy with Oak and Brambles.

shallow_creek.name: Shallow Creek
shallow_creek.description: A fast-moving waterway. Source of stones and silt.

mineral_crust.name: Mineral Crust
mineral_crust.description: A dry, chalky shelf of salt and bone-fragments. Very high resonance.
